turns.user_message: I’ve noticed lately that my usual morning routine feels completely off. I used to breeze through my to-do list without a second thought, but now I just stare at the screen for ages. It’s only been a couple of weeks, but the drag is already noticeable. I catch myself sighing over simple tasks that never bothered me before. I just wish my brain would snap back to its normal pace.

That weird sluggishness I noticed a few weeks ago has definitely settled into a heavy routine. I wake up dreading the same repetitive cycle, even though I know I should just push through it. My focus keeps fracturing, and I find myself rereading the same paragraphs over and over without absorbing a single word. It’s frustrating because I know I’m capable of more, but my energy just evaporates the moment I sit down. I keep waiting for it to pass, but it only seems to stick around longer.

I’ve realized that this recent fog has completely taken over my daily rhythm, and it feels impossible to shake off. Every single task now looks like a massive wall that I just don’t have the strength to climb. I sit there for hours trying to force myself into motion, but my mind just goes completely blank. The constant pressure is grinding me down to a point where I barely recognize my own work habits anymore. I feel completely hollowed out, like I’m running on empty while the demands keep piling up around me.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This just started recently.
